message:
feat(parity): NodeView.data() helper for Graph and DiGraph

Closes franken_networkx-yjiv.

Adds data() method to NodeView and DiNodeView that returns a view
iterating over (node, attrs) pairs, matching NetworkX's signature:
  G.nodes.data()                      # all attrs
  G.nodes.data('color')               # specific attr
  G.nodes.data('missing', default='NA')  # with default

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"franken_networkx-6nr0","title":"Public clustering wrapper lacks NetworkX nodes and weight contract","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T05:24:04.533366101Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T05:29:56.083772139Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T05:29:56.081666764Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-6orz","title":"parity: MultiGraph.to_directed() should preserve NetworkX edge and key order","description":"MultiGraph.to_directed() currently materializes directed multiedges in a different observable order than upstream NetworkX. On insertion sequence a-b(k1), a-b(k2), b-c(k3), d-a(k4), FrankenNetworkX starts with d->a(k4) and delays a->d(k4), while NetworkX preserves the expected insertion-derived order [('a','b','k1'), ('a','b','k2'), ('a','d','k4'), ...]. Match the upstream edge/key iteration order contract for MultiGraph.to_directed().","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:17:28.567673411Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:17:28.567673411Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"franken_networkx-6r4x","title":"parity: Graph and DiGraph EdgeView should support set algebra","description":"Graph.edges and DiGraph.edges currently expose edge views that are not set-like. In FrankenNetworkX, operations such as G.edges | {('b', 'c')}, G.edges & {('a', 'b')}, G.edges - {('a', 'b')}, and G.edges ^ {('a', 'b'), ('b', 'c')} raise TypeError, while upstream NetworkX returns the expected edge sets. Match the upstream EdgeView set-algebra contract on Graph and DiGraph.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:50:53.633905734Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:50:53.633905734Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

In FrankenNetworkX, operations such as G.edges | {('b', 'c')}, G.edges & {('a', 'b')}, G.edges - {('a', 'b')}, and G.edges ^ {('a', 'b'), ('b', 'c')} raise TypeError, while upstream NetworkX returns the expected edge sets. Match the upstream EdgeView set-algebra contract on Graph and DiGraph.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:50:53.633905734Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:26:18.327643520Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:26:18.325698038Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 66b205e","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"franken_networkx-6sz1","title":"Port parse_gml off NetworkX fallback","description":"Route parse_gml through the local GML reader, add no-fallback tests, and update coverage counts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-18T03:13:27.706924639Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T05:28:10.593842773Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T05:28:10.591210120Z","close_reason":"Ported parse_gml through local read_gml with no-fallback tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-6t7","title":"Structural: k-edge augmentation, k-edge components, spectral bisection","description":"## Scope
Implement 5 structural decomposition functions:
- k_edge_augmentation(G, k, avail=None, weight=None, partial=False) — find minimum-weight set of edges to add to make G k-edge-connected. For k=1: connect components via minimum spanning forest of complement. For k=2: use ear decomposition to find bridges and add parallel paths. For general k: iterative approach. Returns list of (u,v) edges to add.
- k_edge_components(G, k) — partition nodes into maximally k-edge-connected components. For k=1: connected_components. For k=2: biconnected_components minus bridges. General k: use max-flow.
- k_edge_subgraphs(G, k) — yield subgraphs of k-edge-connected components (each is a subgraph induced by a component).
- spectral_bisection(G, weight=None) — partition graph into two roughly equal parts using the sign of the Fiedler vector (second-smallest eigenvector of Laplacian). Nodes with positive Fiedler component go to one side, negative to the other. This is the basis of spectral clustering.
- find_induced_nodes(G, s, d) — find all nodes at exactly distance d from node s via BFS. Returns set of nodes.

## Dependencies
- spectral_bisection depends on existing fiedler_vector implementation
- k_edge_augmentation for k>=3 may depend on flow algorithms (already implemented)

## Performance
- k_edge_augmentation: O(k * max_flow_cost) for general k
- spectral_bisection: O(n^2) for eigendecomposition, O(n+m) for sign partitioning
- find_induced_nodes: O(n+m) BFS

## Test Requirements
- k_edge_augmentation: verify augmented graph has edge_connectivity >= k
- k_edge_components: verify each component has internal edge connectivity >= k
- spectral_bisection: verify partition is roughly balanced, cut size is reasonable
- find_induced_nodes: verify all returned nodes are at exactly distance d
- NX cross-validation on path, cycle, complete, and Petersen graphs","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-22T17:34:38.864656076Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T23:47:40.732277451Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T23:47:40.731715185Z","close_reason":"Implemented k_edge_components, k_edge_subgraphs, spectral_bisection, find_induced_nodes, k_edge_augmentation.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["algorithms"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"franken_networkx-6t7","depends_on_id":"franken_networkx-ak4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T17:42:04.409020795Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
